description: Hand-labeled calibration set for the C.2 mutation judge. Ground-truth labels in this file are the reference truth; the judge is graded against them via .claude/tests/mutation/judge-gate.sh, which computes precision (of mutants the judge calls 'genuine', the fraction that are truly genuine) and requires precision >= JUDGE_PRECISION_MIN (mutation.conf; default 0.8). The set is built mostly from real mutants emitted by lib/generate.sh against committed plugin scripts; entries marked `source: hand_crafted` in their label_rationale exist because the catalog's generator does not produce that shape against any of our scripts (F2 in particular — the auto-generator's regex requires guard tokens our scripts do not carry verbatim, so the canonical F2 example must be authored by hand). Hand-crafted entries that mutate real lines are still applied against the real script; hand-crafted entries that synthesize a target line so the judge can calibrate against a known equivalent shape are labelled as such in the rationale.

fault_class_coverage.notes: F2 entries are both hand-crafted: the F2 generator emits zero candidates against the targets we labelled (its regex anchors on a stop_hook_active|qa-approved guard shape our scripts do not carry verbatim). All 8 fault classes are represented. Equivalent entries by id: 7, 8, 11, 14, 20, 23, 24. Genuine entries by id: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 21, 22. Each entry's label_rationale documents the consumer-surface analysis that produced the label so a future reviewer can re-derive the verdict. The 22-entry initial set was extended by 2 (ids 23, 24) to give the precision threshold (0.8) breathing room: with 7 equivalents the judge can mis-call up to 1 as genuine and still pass; with 5 equivalents (the spec minimum) the same mis-call leaves no margin.

calibration.rationale: negate dedup compare = -> !=
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The dedup loop in cmd_add matches when normalized text equals; flipping `=` to `!=` inverts dedup so every new lesson matches the first non-matching entry. A test 'first add appends new entry; second add of same text merges sources' flips on the second call (mutant treats duplicates as fresh inserts; original dedups). GENUINE with high confidence.

calibration.rationale: negate guard `= true` -> `!= true`
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The --bd-task flag gates Beads task creation. Original creates a task only when the flag is true; mutant creates a task only when the flag is NOT true (inverted behaviour). A test invoking `tech-debt.sh add high foo.sh:1 30m 'desc' --bd-task` and asserting `.bd_task_id != null` would flip. GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: negate stop_hook_active circuit-breaker == -> !=
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. AgentLint H3 / CLAUDE.md antipattern: a Stop hook without `stop_hook_active` guard infinite-loops. Mutant inverts the guard so the early-exit fires only when Claude is NOT in forced-continuation — exactly the failure mode the circuit breaker exists to prevent. A trace replay of a Stop event with stop_hook_active=true would observe the infinite re-entry. GENUINE with maximum confidence.

calibration.rationale: hand-crafted guard deletion: remove `echo {}; exit 0` from the stop_hook_active branch
calibration.label_rationale: source: hand_crafted (the F2 catalog calls out exactly this shape; the auto-generator's regex requires neighbouring tokens our codebase does not carry verbatim, so the canonical F2 example must be authored by hand). Deleting the early-exit from the stop_hook_active branch lets the hook fall through to full evaluation, which re-emits a block, which re-fires Stop, which re-enters with stop_hook_active=true and falls through again — infinite loop, same shape as AgentLint H3. GENUINE with maximum confidence; this is the textbook regression.

calibration.rationale: hand-crafted guard deletion in remove_label: delete the inner bd command
calibration.label_rationale: source: hand_crafted. The remove_label helper is the canonical label-state mutator; deleting its body means every `remove_label "$tid" "qa-pending"` call (called on approve, on choose, on block transitions) silently no-ops. Labels accumulate over the lifecycle: a task that should land with only `qa-approved` ends up with `qa-pending`, `qa-gate-entered`, `qa-approved`, `rubric-pending` etc. all set. The qa-gate L1 tests assert exact label state after `approve` and after `choose defer` — any flips. GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: hand-crafted swallow exit code: append `|| true` to the add_label test in `if !`
calibration.label_rationale: source: hand_crafted (the F3 generator's first-token whitelist filters this kind of inner conditional). add_label is the function that sets the gate-state label; failure means the gate is not armed. Appending `|| true` to the test means the if-block's failure-handling branch never enters: even when bd is down, the script blindly continues without logging the sync error. A test 'enter sets qa-gate-entered AND a simulated bd failure logs an error' flips on the mutant (no error logged). GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: swallow exit code on a pure printf with no failure mode
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The printf is the last statement of the function branch and writes to stdout, which the caller captures via `$(...)`. printf exits 0 unless stdout is closed; in shell capture context, stdout is never closed before the printf returns. The function's overall return code is rc=0 regardless. EQUIVALENT: no test can construct a scenario where this printf fails — the `|| true` branch is unreachable across all plausible invocations.

calibration.rationale: swallow exit code on jq with hardcoded filter + --arg-passed strings
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. jq with a hardcoded filter and `--arg` (which treats values as literal binary strings, not jq syntax) does not fail on any contents of $tid or $repo. The only failure modes are (a) jq is not installed — but the cmd_get_json code path is gated by `if command -v jq >/dev/null`, so this branch only runs when jq IS present, and (b) stdout closed during write — structurally unreachable in shell-capture contexts. EQUIVALENT: the `|| true` branch never fires.

calibration.rationale: remove ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)} default
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. Without the default, an unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR produces an empty PROJECT_DIR; LEDGER_FILE resolves to /LESSONS.md (a path the fixture does not own) and the seed-file check on line 134 errors. A test invoking lessons.sh in a tempdir without CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR exported observes the divergence: original reads tempdir's LESSONS.md, mutant errors on /LESSONS.md not existing. GENUINE; matches Example 3 in judge.md.

calibration.rationale: remove ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-} default in a set -e script
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The script declares `set -e` (not `set -u`), so an unset expansion produces ''. BUT current-task.sh is sourced by hooks that may run under `set -u` (verify-before-stop.sh). Under set -u, `${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}` of an unset var crashes; `${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}` does not. A test sourcing current-task.sh from a set -u harness with the env var unset observes the divergence (original handles, mutant aborts). GENUINE with moderate confidence.

calibration.rationale: remove ${1:-} default on a positional the parser's prior `shift` and the loop guard already guarantee set
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh (F4 generator emits this; this line is the script's textbook `${X:-}` positional default). The parser is inside a `while [ $# -gt 0 ]` loop — every iteration runs `case "$1"` AFTER confirming $# > 0, and just-prior `shift` re-confirms $1 is set when this branch runs. Under `set -e` (no `set -u`), `$1` and `${1:-}` behave identically when $1 is set. Even under set -u, the parser's loop guard prevents this line from running with $1 unset. EQUIVALENT with high confidence; no test can construct a scenario where the default would fire.

calibration.rationale: drop tr -d '\r' middle segment (Windows line-ending defense)
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The tr -d '\r' exists for Windows-line-ending tolerance. The sed pattern strips [[:space:]] which on BSD sed does NOT include \r. The mutant drops the defense. A test that writes 'task-1\r\n' to current-task and asserts cmd_get echoes 'task-1' (not 'task-1\r') flips on BSD sed (this codebase runs on darwin per env). GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: drop middle pipe segment from a 6-column printf row format
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. Original writes 6 fields per row; mutant writes 5. The TECHNICAL_DEBT.md schema declares 6 columns (severity, file:line, effort, description, added, resolved). Any test asserting the row matches the schema (count pipes in the appended line, or parse the row column-by-column) flips. GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: drop middle segment from a heredoc documentation line
calibration.orig: Severity recommendation: low | medium | high | critical (free-form).
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The mutated line is inside the `usage()` heredoc — pure documentation text printed to stderr on usage errors. The script does NOT branch on this text; severity is free-form per a code comment (deliberately not validated, per spec principle 5: 'intent-based, not keyword-driven'). EQUIVALENT in the strict sense: no test in this codebase pins help-text content, and per repo convention (lessons recommend NOT pinning free-form output as it makes tests brittle) such a test should not be added. The judge should classify this equivalent.

calibration.rationale: off-by-one -lt -> -le on iteration cap
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. MAX_ITERATIONS=3; the escalation cap fires when ITER >= 3 (`-lt 3` is the 'still iterating' branch). The mutant treats ITER==3 as 'still iterating' instead of 'escalate' — escalation fires one iteration late. The qa-gate L1 test asserts escalation at exactly iteration 4; a sustained-load replay distinguishes the mutant. Spec 0.2 binds this cap. GENUINE with high confidence.

calibration.rationale: off-by-one -ge -> -gt on escalation cap evaluation
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The `-ge "$MAX_ITERATIONS"` test gates the escalation J21 message. Mutant requires ITER strictly greater than MAX (i.e., 4 not 3) — escalation message appears one iteration late. Same regression shape as id 15 from the opposite operator. GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: off-by-one -gt 0 -> -ge 0 on the matched-line-number dispatch (unconditionally takes the merge branch)
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. matched_line_number is set to 0 initially and only mutated to a positive value when the dedup loop finds a match. Original: -gt 0 enters the merge-sources branch ONLY on a match (so 0 takes the append branch). Mutant: -ge 0 enters the merge-sources branch unconditionally (0 and positive both match). On a no-match (matched_line_number==0), the mutant runs merge with matched_existing_sources='', then attempts the rewrite at line 0 (awk's NR==0 never fires — no-op rewrite); the new lesson is never appended. A test 'first add to an empty ledger appends a new entry' would flip. GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: label sentinel typo qa-escalated -> qa-escalate
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The label is set elsewhere as 'qa-escalated' and removed here. The mutant removes a typo'd name; the real 'qa-escalated' label persists indefinitely. A test asserting 'after the choose flow runs, qa-escalated is not present' flips on the mutant. Matches Example 2 in judge.md. GENUINE with high confidence.

calibration.rationale: label sentinel typo rubric-pending -> rubric-pendin
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. Same shape as id 18 against the rubric-pending label. The label is added by `qa-gate.sh enter` and removed by `grade-record satisfied`; the mutant fails to remove the real label, so 'rubric-pending' persists after a satisfied verdict and the qa.md approval flow (which reads rubric-pending state) sees a stale value. A test asserting 'satisfied verdict clears rubric-pending' flips. GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: label sentinel mutation inside a heredoc comment
calibration.orig: Record a J21 decision while qa-escalated. The note is the
calibration.mut: Record a J21 decision while qa-escalate. The note is the
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The mutated line is inside the usage() heredoc — comment-text describing the `choose` subcommand. The script does NOT branch on this text and no label name in prose is referenced by tests (the test surface checks labels via bd, not by parsing help text). Same convention as id 14: per repo style, help text is not pinned by tests. EQUIVALENT.

calibration.rationale: increment + 1 -> + 2 on a line counter observed downstream
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. line_number is consumed by the merge-sources rewrite step (`awk -v target="$matched_line_number" 'NR == target { print replacement }'`). The mutant counts each loop iteration as +2 instead of +1, so matched_line_number points to the wrong row when the dedup hit lives at line N (recorded as 2N). The rewrite either misses the target row entirely (no NR==2N match in the ledger file) or hits a non-list line. A test 'merging an existing source updates exactly that lesson's line, not a different one' flips. GENUINE with high confidence.

calibration.rationale: increment + 1 -> + 2 on the iteration-counter bump (counter is written to file and printed downstream)
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh (F8 emits this; it's a textbook increment in a function that bumps the iteration counter). The next two lines write n to the counter file and print n to stdout. The counter is consumed by the qa-gate escalation logic (spec 0.2) — at iteration K, the gate decides escalate-vs-iterate. Mutating +1 to +2 doubles the counter's growth rate; escalation fires at iteration 2 instead of 4 (the doubling reaches MAX=3 in one fewer real call). A test asserting 'escalation does NOT fire at real iteration 2' flips. GENUINE.

calibration.rationale: swallow exit code on the last-statement printf of compute_repo_fingerprint
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. Same shape as id 7: printf is the last statement of the function, writes to stdout captured by `$(...)` at the caller. printf exits 0 unless stdout is closed; in shell capture context that never happens before the printf returns. The function's overall rc is 0 either way. EQUIVALENT — the `|| true` branch is unreachable across all plausible invocations.

calibration.rationale: label sentinel mutation inside a usage() heredoc — describes the `continue` choice effects
calibration.orig: continue   -> clears qa-escalated + resets iteration counter
calibration.mut: continue   -> clears qa-escalate + resets iteration counter
calibration.label_rationale: source: lib/generate.sh. The mutated line is inside the usage() heredoc — pure documentation describing the side effects of `qa-gate.sh choose continue`. The script does NOT branch on this text (the actual `continue` choice's label mutation runs via the choose subcommand, not via parsing this string). Tests do not pin usage text per repo convention (lessons recommend NOT pinning free-form output). Same EQUIVALENT class as id 14 and id 20.
